text.title: CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md Convention Rules
text.summary: CLAUDE.md one-liner rule, AGENTS.md strict shape, line-cap enforcement, and the gotchas section requirement.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md must contain exactly one line: @AGENTS.md. Nothing else. Any content added to CLAUDE.md belongs in AGENTS.md.
rule.rationale: CLAUDE.md is an entrypoint, not a doc. The @AGENTS.md import directive tells Claude Code to auto-load AGENTS.md. Putting content here bypasses the convention and creates a parallel source of truth.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md target length: 20-80 lines. Hard cap: 120 lines. If it exceeds 80, split detailed content into .agents/ reference docs and link from AGENTS.md.
rule.rationale: Routing-first principle: agents narrow search per directory level. A 500-line AGENTS.md negates load-on-demand and wastes context budget on every load.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md must include: Summary (1 sentence), file table (name → purpose), key types/exports, common commands (exact as in package.json/Makefile), and Gotchas.
The Gotchas section is mandatory and is the highest-value content. Extract from: TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK comments in source and recent fix commit messages.
rule.rationale: Gotchas correct training-data assumptions unique to this codebase. Without them, agents reproduce the same bugs documented in fix commits.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md are required in every directory that contains source code — not just repo roots, but also subpackages, module folders, test dirs.
Skip only: empty __init__.py-only dirs with no logic, generated/vendored dirs (node_modules/, dist/), and pure-data dirs (assets/, fixtures/).
Reference commands exactly as they exist in package.json or Makefile. Verify with grep before writing. Never invent commands that look plausible.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated commands are the most common silent breakage in AGENTS.md. They look correct and agents execute them, failing silently.
